spec_match.rationale: Runs a real VideoEncoder/VideoDecoder VP8 round-trip with rotation/flip on the source VideoFrame and reads frame.rotation/frame.flip on the decoded side — this is the canonical motivating case from the spec.

interactive.rationale: Two selects plus an `encode → decode → render` button kick a real WebCodecs pipeline; the timeline log narrates each phase with byte counts and probed metadata.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises the spec's motivation case end-to-end and that's exactly what the demo does, including a `did it survive` assertion in the log.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles `WebCodecs not available` and `vp8 unsupported` gracefully. Misses the spec's VideoEncoder active-orientation lock — mid-stream rotation change should throw DataError, never demonstrated. Also no H.264/AV1 codec switch to show codec-specific carriage.

references.rationale: see-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5128855343595520 and the canonical rotation spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only CSS, support pill uses paired accent + --bg-ivory text, no static-card. Canvas pixel colours are inside bitmaps, not CSS, so no contrast rule applies.

openQuestions.title: Add the VideoEncoder DataError demonstration
openQuestions.detail: Per spec, encoder.encode of a frame whose rotation/flip differs from the first encoded frame must throw DataError. The page should add a `change orientation mid-stream` button that demonstrates this — currently the strongest constraint in the spec is invisible.

openQuestions.title: Show codec-specific carriage (H.264, AV1)
openQuestions.detail: VP8 doesn't have a bitstream-level orientation field, so this round-trip currently relies on Chrome's in-process metadata pass-through, not the container. A codec dropdown that includes H.264 (where the SPS could carry it) and AV1 would surface whether the metadata survives transmuxing — a question authors actually ask.

openQuestions.title: Show codedWidth vs displayWidth on the decoded canvas
openQuestions.detail: When rotation is 90/270, decodedFrame.codedWidth and displayWidth differ. The log already prints codedWidth — add displayWidth so the user sees the swap explicitly. Right now the right-hand canvas does the rotation visually but the dimensions story isn't told in numbers.

openQuestions.title: Persist the chunk so user can replay decode
openQuestions.detail: Currently the encoder closes after one frame and the chunk is thrown away. Stashing the chunk + meta and exposing a `decode again with different rotation override` button would teach the spec's `decoder configures rotation` override path.

summary: The strongest of the four — runs a real WebCodecs pipeline end-to-end with an honest fallback when the codec is unsupported. Missing only the spec's most-bitten constraints: the active-orientation lock DataError and codec-level carriage differences.
